text: Did the doctors find out how the woman died?
zh: 医生们查明这位女性的死因了吗?

text: The mummy of an Egyptian woman who died in 800 B.C. has just had an operation.
zh: 一具公元前800年去世的埃及女性木乃伊刚刚接受了一次手术。

text: The mummy is that of Shepenmut who was once a singer in the Temple of Thebes.
zh: 这具木乃伊属于谢潘穆特,她曾是底比斯神庙里的一名歌者。

text: As there were strange marks on the X-ray plates taken of the mummy,
zh: 由于木乃伊的X光片上出现了奇怪的痕迹,

text: doctors have been trying to find out whether the woman died of a rare disease.
zh: 医生们一直在设法查明她是否死于一种罕见疾病。

text: The only way to do this was to operate.
zh: 唯一的办法就是动手术。

text: The operation, which lasted for over four hours,
zh: 这场手术持续了四个多小时,

text: proved to be very difficult because of the hard resin which covered the skin.
zh: 由于覆盖在皮肤上的树脂十分坚硬,手术进行得异常艰难。

text: The doctors removed a section of the mummy and sent it to a laboratory.
zh: 医生们取下木乃伊的一部分,送往实验室化验。

text: They also found something which the X-ray plates did not show: a small wax figure of the god Duamutef.
zh: 他们还发现了X光片上没有显示的东西:一尊杜阿姆泰夫神的小蜡像。

text: This god which has the head of a cow was normally placed inside a mummy.
zh: 这位牛头神像通常被放置在木乃伊体内。

text: The doctors have not yet decided how the woman died.
zh: 医生们至今仍未确定这位女性的死因。

text: They feared that the mummy would fall to pieces when they cut it open,
zh: 他们原本担心切开木乃伊时它会碎裂,

text: but fortunately this has not happened.
zh: 但幸运的是,这种情况并未发生。

text: The mummy successfully survived the operation.
zh: 这具木乃伊成功挺过了这场手术。
